name: Phil Woods
description: American jazz alto saxophonist, clarinetist, bandleader, and composer (1931–2015)
abstract: Philip Wells Woods (November 2, 1931 – September 29, 2015) was an American jazz alto saxophonist, clarinetist, bandleader, and composer.

interviews.title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999
Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I bought everything that had a saxophone on it. And I didn’t know, you know I had everything from of course Charlie Parker, Phil Woods, Ornette Coleman...
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Christine Jensen
about_quotes.evidence: I met him once so I didn’t really get to spend enough time with Phil Woods and I should have.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christine Jensen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: When you went to Russia with Benny Goodman, you were on that first tour? ... so did Phil Woods. ... Phil said, 'Man, what are you taking all those pictures for?' ... I picked Phil Woods over my shoulder, put him over my shoulder and carried him up three flights of stairs.

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: It was Zoot Sims, Tommy Newsom, I think Gene Allen was playing baritone, Phil Woods and Jerry Dodgion was playing second alto. Trombone were Jimmy Knepper, Willie Dennis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had 33 people... Phil Woods... Phil Woods. Okay. What’s he going to do? Ten months. We worked with Nat Cole... Phil’s written a book about all this. Phil’s a great writer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote originally for Phil Woods and recorded it... The players were all available, marvelous players, Art Farmer, Phil Woods... I knew they would be there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998
